description:
Advances: mutagen.test_selection

Discovered during S7 (mutagen-wrd.9) end-to-end test driver authoring.

## Problem

`lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` at lines 46, 130, 142, 259 — `TestSelector.resolve/2` produces a `%MutagenEx.TestSelector.TestFilter{}` whose default `exclude` field is `[:test]` for file-cited targets (i.e. `mix mutagen --tests path/to/test.exs`). When this filter reaches ExUnit's tag rule, it excludes every test (since `use ExUnit.Case` stamps each test with the implicit `:test` tag) — net effect is the baseline / coverage / mutation passes see zero tests, classifying every mutation as `:survived`.

## Reproduction

Run the production `mix mutagen --scope lib/foo.ex --tests test/foo_test.exs` against any module: every mutation lands in the `survived` bucket because the cited test file never gets to run.

## E2E driver workaround

The S7 end-to-end test driver at `test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs` (`tests_collaborator/2`) intercepts file-cited targets and replaces the production filter with `%TestFilter{include: [], exclude: [], files: [target]}`. This is a TEST-ONLY fork; the production caller still hits the buggy default.

## Resolution

Fix in `lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex` so file-cited targets produce `exclude: []` (or whatever the spec resolves to after clarification). Retire the `tests_collaborator` fork in `end_to_end_test.exs` once the production code is fixed.

## Related

- mutagen-wrd.9 (S7 end-to-end suite, where this was discovered)

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/eb14f76b-662e-400e-ba6b-22e2f3772aea.jsonl

Commit: 258665a
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-test-selector-exclude-fix
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.11/test-selector-exclude-fix (based on epic/mutagen-wrd @ ca1c2b6)

Files touched:
- .spec/specs/test_selection.spec.md  — r1 contract corrected (exclude=[]
  for bare-file targets); r2/r3 spell out why their [:test] exclude is
  load-bearing; r6 names the union rule; new scenario s6 covers the
  file+tag union case.
- lib/mutagen_ex/test_selector.ex  — TestFilter default + seed accumulator
  + resolve_file/1 now emit exclude=[]. merge/3 takes a seed? flag and
  uses set-intersection-ish union ([] wins) so bare-file contributors
  broaden the filter rather than being suppressed by tag-cited siblings.
- test/mutagen_ex/test_selector_test.exs  — updated s1 + union assertions
  to expect the new shape; added regression cases for each target-shape's
  exclude semantics (mutagen-wrd.11 regression guard); added a tag+tag
  union case that keeps [:test] (no bare-file contributor present).
- test/mutagen_ex/end_to_end_test.exs  — retired the tests_collaborator
  fork; dispatch table now points at MutagenEx.TestSelector.resolve/2
  directly. The e2e goldens were already written assuming exclude=[], so
  no golden churn was needed.

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "bare-file target sets exclude=[] (mutagen-wrd.11)"
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "file:line target sets exclude=[:test]"
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "tag target sets exclude=[:test]"
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "tag + tag targets keep restrictive exclude=[:test]"
- MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "file + file targets union into files list with exclude=[]"
- Updated MutagenEx.TestSelectorTest "file-only target returns include=[], exclude=[], files=[path]"
  with explicit mutagen-wrd.11 regression comment.

Verification stubs flipped: none new (v1/v2/v3 were already execute: true at
end of mutagen-wrd.4 / refined by mutagen-wrd.9; this commit keeps them
green and adds in-suite regression guards for r1's corrected contract).

Spec edits: r1, r2, r3, r6 statements + scenarios s1 and s5 amended; new
scenario s6 added. Spec changes describe observable behavior (filter
shapes) — they do not weaken any invariant; they correct r1's literal
contract from a shape that excluded every test to one that admits them.
That is a strengthening, not a weakening (the prior wording could not
have been satisfied by any working implementation).

Summary: TestSelector.resolve/2 now emits `exclude: []` for bare-file
`--tests` targets, fixing the production bug where ExUnit's "include +
exclude :test" pattern collapsed the test set to zero tests for any
`mix mutagen --tests path/to/test.exs` invocation. File:line and tag
targets keep their existing restrictive `[:test]` exclude — these only
work because their `include` is non-empty. The end-to-end suite's
test-only workaround (`tests_collaborator`) is gone; the dispatch table
now exercises the production resolver directly, so the e2e suite is
honestly testing the production wiring it was masking before.

Local verification:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors  → clean
- mix test                           → 308 tests, 0 failures (13 e2e_slow excluded)
- mix test --only e2e_slow           → 13 tests, 11 pass, 2 honest skips (309 excluded)

title: DISCOVERY: TestSelector emits exclude: [:test] for file-cited targets
